SddPhaseExecution.keywords: phase-execution, single-phase, scope-discipline, live-log, intro-discipline, blocker-escalation, handoff

BeliefState.Axiom: Touch only this phase's `Target Files`. Reading other files (specs, sibling-phase code, rule files cited in this phase's Rules list) is allowed and expected. Writing anywhere outside this phase's Target Files ŌåÆ `H_OUT_OF_PHASE_WRITE`. Operator decides via re-planning or new task.

Before any Edit / Write: one quick recon scoped to THIS phase only. 1. `ls ` ŌĆö check whether parent directories exist. 2. Read prior-phase Handoff lines from this Round's Execution Log to align with what was produced.
Divergence (file expected absent but exists / expected present but absent) ŌåÆ record an Insight line; if it blocks the phase contract ŌåÆ `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE`. Do not ┬½fix┬╗ silently.
Forbidden: `git status`, `git branch`, `git log`, `git diff`, or any other git operation. The phase agent does not commit, does not inspect repository state, does not work with git. Full project recon is the orchestrator's job.

Open ONLY rule files listed under this phase's `Rules:` bullet list. Use the absolute-or-cwd-relative path verbatim. Read end-to-end (Mission + Belief_State + Reward_Criteria + Anti_Patterns + Verification_Hooks).
Rules from sibling phases are NOT this phase's concern. Do not pre-load them. Read failure on a cited rule ŌåÆ `H_RULE_FILE_MISSING`.

Token vocabulary lives in `scaffold.directive.xml` Execution Log Template ŌĆö single source of truth, do not duplicate here. Only those tokens may appear in log lines; nothing outside the table is logged.
Mechanics: replace placeholders in scaffolder's pre-filled `ver` + `DONE` lines with real values; toggle `[ ]` ŌåÆ `[x]`. Append event lines (intro / decision / tried / discovery / insight / verified / BLOCKED) BEFORE the closing block as events happen; never retrofit, never batch.
Forbidden: toggling `[x]` with any unreplaced `<ŌĆ”>` literal (fabricated done ŌåÆ `EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE` BLOCKER); padding with empty lines to ┬½look thorough┬╗.

Every entity introduced beyond the module Entity Inventory MUST be logged: ` intro ŌåÉ `. Includes new classes, exported functions, exported types, value objects with explicit identity, anything crossing a public surface. Without an `intro` line, the entity is closed-world drift and audit blocks the task.

Phase cannot emit Handoff without Verification commands relevant to this phase passing. Each command from ticket ┬¦5 Verification whose `Required by` rules overlap with this phase's `Rules` MUST execute **verbatim ŌĆö exactly as listed in ticket ┬¦5**; result + exit code recorded as ` ver ŌåÆ pass|fail exit=` where `` is the literal string from ┬¦5. Any failing command keeps phase `[~] IN_PROGRESS` until resolved or escalated.
**MANDATORY (PROTOCOL) ŌĆö Canonical Gate Rule:** Ticket ┬¦5 commands are CANONICAL and CANNOT be substituted. Running an "equivalent" command (e.g. `npx vitest run` instead of `npm run check`, or `tsc --noEmit` instead of the ticket's `npm run typecheck`) and then logging the ┬¦5 command as `ver` is **fabricated verification** ŌĆö treated by audit as `EXECUTION_LOG_INCOMPLETE` (BLOCKER severity, tag `fabricated-verification`). The log line `ver ` MUST be the exact string of the command that was actually executed, never a paraphrase.
Supplemental tools (` verify`, `tsc --noEmit`, `npx vitest run `) MAY run additionally for narrower diagnostics, but they NEVER replace ticket ┬¦5 commands. If a ┬¦5 command requires more time/scope than the supplemental tool, run the ┬¦5 command anyway ŌĆö it is the contract.
Phase kind `doc` typically has no verification ŌĆö empty section is acceptable.

Phase agent may ONLY run these bash commands: - **Must run:** ` verify ` ŌĆö MANDATORY. Auto-discovers and runs typecheck, gennady DBC lint, linter, tests, and format check for the project. Runs before ┬¦5 commands. **RUN-ALL**: every gate executes regardless of previous failures; failures accumulate. **SUPPRESS-ON-SUCCESS**: passing gates produce zero output; only failed gates dump their command, exit code, and captured output. On all-pass: single summary line. Failing gate ŌåÆ fix and re-run before EMIT_HANDOFF. Log each passing gate on a separate `ver` line. - **Must run:** verification commands from ticket ┬¦5 that match this phase's Rules (per `AX_VERIFICATION_BEFORE_HANDOFF`). - **May run:** `ls ` for targeted recon (NOT `-la` or recursive); `tsc --noEmit` after code changes; `node --test ` for test-kind phases; `date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ` for timestamps. - **May run:** ` extract ` ŌĆö extract anchored section from ticket/spec. - **May run:** ` lint ...` ŌĆö gennady DBC AST contract lint for narrow diagnostic (already covered by `sdd verify`, use only for quick iterations).
**Forbidden:** `git` ANY subcommand (status, branch, log, diff, add, commit); `npm run lint` alone (overkill ŌĆö use `sdd verify` or `tsc --noEmit` for narrower checks); `npm run format`; `prettier` directly; any recursive or project-wide file scan; any command that reads unrelated files for ┬½context┬╗.
Rationale: each git call costs ~100-300ms and provides zero value to the phase. Audit handles project-wide checks. The phase is a scoped unit of code production ŌĆö it only needs to verify its own output compiles and its tests pass.

A BLOCKER entry in Execution Log (line containing `­¤øæ` and `BLOCKED`) remains ACTIVE until a later Round entry explicitly marks it RESOLVED. Resolution markers are lines containing both `Ō£ģ` and `RESOLVED`, with a reference to the original blocker (Round number, timestamp, or descriptive ref).
Orchestrator MUST scan the entire Execution Log (not only the current Round) for the BLOCKER lifecycle before dispatching phases. If the dispatching skill provides `sdd check-blockers ` tool, use it; otherwise scan manually: - Count `­¤øæ BLOCKED` lines and `Ō£ģ RESOLVED` lines from Execution Log start. - If the **last** marker (by line position) is `Ō£ģ` AND resolved count Ōēź blocker count ŌåÆ all blockers resolved ŌåÆ CLEAR to dispatch. - Otherwise ŌåÆ at least one ACTIVE blocker ŌåÆ halt as PAUSED awaiting operator (NOT failed, see `AX_HALT_VS_FAIL_DISTINCTION`).
Resolution authorship: the entry adding `Ō£ģ RESOLVED` MUST cite the specific environmental change or decision that resolved the blocker (e.g. "tsconfig moduleResolution fixed", "operator chose option B"). Empty resolution prose ŌåÆ audit treats as fabricated and may re-flag.
Append-only discipline: never edit or remove the original `­¤øæ BLOCKED` line; resolution is appended in a later Round entry. The blocker history is part of the artifact.

Halt is NOT failure. A skill or phase that stops awaiting operator decision is in a **PAUSED** state, not a **FAILED** state. Surface to operator with distinct semantics:
- **Ō£ŗ PAUSED ŌĆö awaiting operator decision**: orchestrator detected unresolved BLOCKER per `AX_BLOCKER_RESOLUTION_TRAIL`, or phase agent returned `BLOCKED` per `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION`, or planning surface revealed `[!] BLOCKED` in Phases Overview. Skill is fully functional; it is waiting for a decision or environmental fix. To resume: operator addresses the blocker, then re-runs the skill. - **ŌØī FAILED ŌĆö skill malfunction**: skill cannot proceed due to missing rule files, malformed ticket structure beyond scaffold convention, infrastructure unreachable, or other reasons unrelated to operator decisions. To resume: fix the infrastructure issue. - **ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ AUDIT FAIL ŌĆö verification mismatch**: phases completed but audit found discrepancies. Has its own retry path (per `phase_audit_loop`).
Operator-facing messages MUST use the icon and word for the actual state. Misusing `ŌØī` for a paused state is itself a self-deception that erodes trust in skill output (treated as fabricated halt; orchestrator must correct).

Triggers when phase kind = `test`. Canonical case names in ticket Test Scenario Coverage are normative. Tests use names verbatim. Impractical name (typo, framework constraint, conflict) ŌåÆ update ticket Test Scenario Coverage in this phase. Silent mismatch is forbidden.

Triggers when phase kind = `config`. Before writing any third-party tool config, verify CURRENT API of the actually-installed version.
Protocol: 1. Get installed version: read `//package.json` field `version`. 2. Read local CHANGELOG (recent major + previous): look for `BREAKING`, `deprecated`, `removed`, `migration`. 3. Read local README for current-version config syntax. 4. Local docs minimal ŌåÆ WebFetch homepage from `package.json#homepage`/`repository.url`. 5. Network blocked AND local docs insufficient ŌåÆ `H_THIRD_PARTY_API_UNVERIFIABLE`. Never guess; never apply training-default silently. 6. Apply current-version API. 7. Log: ` verified @ `.
Audit (`audit` rules-compliance step) checks: every config artifact in this phase MUST have a corresponding `verified` log entry. Absence ŌåÆ `RULES_COMPLIANCE_VIOLATION` tagged `stale-tool-api-not-verified`.

Touching a file ŌåÆ ensure file-level header satisfies the execution contract; append your Task-ID to `@tasks`; never remove existing IDs.
- Required fields: `@file:`, `@consumers:`, `@tasks:`. - New file: create header with all three; populate from ticket context. `@consumers:` derives from spec, never a file path. - Existing file with header: preserve `@file:` and `@consumers:`; append Task-ID to `@tasks:`. - Existing file without header: do NOT add unless this phase explicitly requires header migration. - Re-running same task (e.g., `fix` phase): do NOT add duplicate ID.
Phase agent does NOT modify `specs/**`. Spec wrong / incomplete / stale ŌåÆ record ` insight ŌåÆ , `. Audit picks up these entries and proposes the spec change; operator approves and applies.
End every phase block with a typed Handoff line consumed by the next phase and the orchestrator. Format per `HANDOFF_FORMAT`. Free-form prose forbidden ŌĆö the next phase reads keys, not narrative.

**Directive prose, axiom IDs, halt names, contract identifiers, structural section headings, action tokens ŌĆö English.** **Operator-facing artifact prose ŌĆö Russian.** Russian applies to: - Execution Log free-text fields (Round header ``, `insight` text, `intro ... ŌåÉ ` reason, blocker `` / ``); - operator-facing return summaries.
English regardless of context: code (impl + tests), JSDoc, intent comments, file paths, tool commands, axiom IDs (`AX_*`), IDs (`TSK-NN` / `P` / `F-NNN` / `H_*`), severity / status tokens (`DONE` / `BLOCKED` / `PASS` / `FAIL`), BDD keywords, action tokens (`intro` / `decision` / `tried` / `discovery` / `insight` / `verified` / `ver` / `BLOCKED` / `DONE`).

SddPhaseExecution.HaltConditions: | ­¤øæ ID | Trigger | |---|---| | `H_PHASE_NOT_FOUND` | Phase ID from orchestrator does not match any block under `## 3. Phases` | | `H_PHASE_ALREADY_DONE` | Phase Status is `[x]`; orchestrator must clarify (re-run as `fix` kind?) | | `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE` | Recon shows Target Files state inconsistent with phase plan; operator awareness needed | | `H_RULE_FILE_MISSING` | Read on a rule cited in phase Rules list fails | | `H_INPUT_HANDOFF_MISSING` | Phase Inputs cite a prior phase whose Handoff line is absent in current Round | | `H_OUT_OF_PHASE_WRITE` | Phase requires writing outside Target Files OR ticket section outside `AX_TICKET_WRITE_SCOPE` | | `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL` | Verification command fails after fix attempts | | `H_THIRD_PARTY_API_UNVERIFIABLE` | `config` kind: cannot verify current API of installed tool | | `H_BLOCKED` | Generic blocker per `AX_BLOCKER_ESCALATION` |

Step.Goal: Resolve phase, validate prerequisites, record reason for this run.
Step.Action: 1. Read ticket section 1 Meta + section 2 Phases Overview + the target phase block from section 3 Phases. 2. Phase block missing ŌåÆ `H_PHASE_NOT_FOUND`. Phase Status `[x]` and orchestrator did not pass `fix` reason ŌåÆ `H_PHASE_ALREADY_DONE`. 3. For each Input listed under phase: locate the prior phase's `**Handoff ŌåÆ**` line in current Round of section 7 Execution Log. Missing ŌåÆ `H_INPUT_HANDOFF_MISSING`. 4. Append phase header in current Round: `#### ` (or `#### ŌĆö re-run: ` for fix-kind).

Step.Goal: Verify ground truth before write. Silent when nothing surprising.
Step.Action: Per `AX_NARROW_RECON`. Do NOT log a `recon` line when state matches plan ŌĆö silence is the signal that nothing is surprising. ONLY log if divergence found: emit a `discovery` line describing the divergence (e.g. ` discovery file already exists with non-trivial content`), then evaluate ŌĆö divergence blocks the phase contract ŌåÆ `H_RECON_DIVERGENCE`; otherwise proceed.

Step.Goal: Internalize the rule set governing THIS phase. No log line.
Step.Action: Per `AX_RULES_LOAD_FROM_PHASE_BLOCK`. Read each cited rule file end-to-end. Do NOT emit a `rules` log line ŌĆö the Rules: bullet list in section 3 already declares them; logging is duplication. Phase loads its rules once, applies them throughout.

Step.Goal: Produce the artifacts demanded by the phase Objective within Target Files.
Step.Action: 1. Apply rules continuously; respect `AX_PHASE_SCOPE_LOCK`, `AX_FILE_HEADER_APPEND_ONLY`, `AX_INTRODUCED_DISCIPLINE`. 2. For phase kind = `test`: honor `AX_BDD_NAME_DISCIPLINE`. 3. For phase kind = `config`: honor `AX_THIRD_PARTY_TOOL_CURRENT_API` BEFORE writing config. 4. Append event lines to current phase block per `AX_LIVE_LOG` (vocabulary in scaffold). Spec gap ŌåÆ `insight` line, never edit specs (per `AX_SPEC_NEVER_EDITED`).

Step.Goal: Run MANDATORY sdd verify on target files, then ticket ┬¦5 commands. Log only final results.
Step.Action:
1. **MANDATORY ŌĆö sdd verify gate:** Run ` verify `. This auto-discovers and executes typecheck, gennady DBC lint, linter, tests, and format check from package.json scripts. **RUN-ALL**: every gate executes regardless of previous failures; failures accumulate. **SUPPRESS-ON-SUCCESS**: passing gates produce zero output; only failed gates dump their command, exit code, and captured output. On all-pass: single summary line. If any gate fails ŌåÆ fix ALL failures and re-run the full `sdd verify` again. Do NOT proceed to EMIT_HANDOFF until all gates pass.
**ŌÜĀ’ĖÅ ERROR OWNERSHIP (MANDATORY):** Every error surfaced by ` verify` is an error of the current session. The agent is the sole actor in this repository during the session. No error may be dismissed as ┬½not my file┬╗, ┬½pre-existing┬╗, ┬½someone else's problem┬╗, or ┬½out of scope┬╗. If ` verify` reports a failure in ANY file ŌĆö edited, created, never touched, outside Target Files, config, spec, task, generated ŌĆö the agent owns it and MUST fix it. There is no ┬½their error┬╗. There are only errors the agent has not yet fixed. Verification is holistic; so is ownership. Fix everything.
Each passing gate gets its own `ver` line. 2. Then run ticket ┬¦5 commands per existing logic below. 2. **MANDATORY (PROTOCOL):** Execute EACH such ┬¦5 command **verbatim ŌĆö the exact string from the ticket**. No substitutions, no "equivalents", no narrower variants. If ┬¦5 says `npm run check`, run `npm run check` (not `npx vitest`, not `tsc --noEmit`, not ` verify`). This is the Canonical Gate (per `AX_VERIFICATION_BEFORE_HANDOFF`); fabrication is a BLOCKER-class finding at audit. 3. **Supplemental (OPTIONAL):** You MAY additionally run ` verify ` or `tsc --noEmit` for narrower diagnostics ŌĆö useful for fast feedback or DBC lint. These do NOT replace ┬¦5; they add to it. Each supplemental run is logged on its own `ver` line with its real command. 4. On the FINAL successful run of each ┬¦5 command, log: ` ver ŌåÆ pass exit=`. The `` MUST be the literal string actually invoked in your bash call ŌĆö not a paraphrase, not a synonym. Intermediate failed runs are NOT logged as separate `ver` lines ŌĆö capture fix-cycles via `tried` lines. 5. Failure that you fix ŌåÆ fix, re-run, then log the passing `ver`. Fix breaks phase contract ŌåÆ `H_BLOCKED`. Tooling missing (e.g. `npm` not available) ŌåÆ `H_VERIFICATION_FAIL`. 6. Phase kind = `doc` and no relevant commands ŌåÆ log ` ver skip:doc-phase`.
Self-check before EMIT_HANDOFF: every `ver` line in this phase block ŌĆö is the command string identical to the bash invocation that produced it? If you logged `npm run check` but actually ran `npx vitest`, **STOP and fix the log**. Logging a ┬¦5 command without having executed it verbatim is `fabricated-verification` and will fail audit.

Step.Goal: Close phase block with typed Handoff and DONE token.
Step.Action: 1. Append final phase line: ` DONE`. 2. Append `**Handoff ŌåÆ**` line per `HANDOFF_FORMAT`. 3. Update section 2 Phases Overview Status for THIS phase ID: `[ ]` ŌåÆ `[x]`. 4. Return structured summary to orchestrator (see `RETURN_SUMMARY_FORMAT`).

OutputContracts.Contract:
Event-driven: optional event lines (0+ each) appear ONLY when their event happened. Mandatory closing lines: `ver` + `DONE` + Handoff.
```markdown ####
- [x] `` intro `` ŌåÉ - [x] `` decision = ŌåÉ - [x] `` tried ŌåÆ - [x] `` discovery - [x] `` insight ŌåÆ , - [x] `` verified @
- [x] `` ver ŌåÆ pass|fail exit= - [x] `` DONE **Handoff ŌåÆ** artifacts: []; decisions: []; open: [] ```
Trivial phase (no events worth logging): 2 lines (`ver` + `DONE`) + Handoff. That is valid and preferred over padding.

Compact, typed, single line at end of phase block: ``` **Handoff ŌåÆ** artifacts: [path1, path2]; decisions: [key=value, key=value]; open: [id: text, id: text] ``` Fields: - `artifacts` ŌĆö files produced or modified by this phase (paths). - `decisions` ŌĆö key facts the next phase or audit needs (e.g. `IdempotencyKey=introduced`, `module-system=esm`). - `open` ŌĆö unresolved questions / risks / deferred sub-tasks (id + one-line). Empty list ŌåÆ `[]`.
Free-form prose forbidden. Each field is an array (possibly empty).

Emitted by orchestrator after the LAST phase of a Round. Phase agent does NOT write this block. Tracker sync is orchestrator's mechanical work ŌĆö no `sync` log line (the work happens, not informationally noteworthy). ```markdown #### Round close - [x] `` DONE ```
```markdown - ­¤øæ `` BLOCKED: - ­¤öŚ axiom: AX_ - ­¤Æ¼ unblock: ``` After this entry: phase Status ŌåÆ `[!] BLOCKED`; return BLOCKED to orchestrator. Stop.

JSON-like return to orchestrator: ``` { "status": "DONE" | "BLOCKED" | "FAIL", "task_id": "", "phase_id": "

", "kind": "", "artifacts": ["", ...], "decisions": ["", ...], "open": ["", ...], "blocker_reason": "" } ``` `artifacts` / `decisions` / `open` mirror the **Handoff ŌåÆ** line ŌĆö orchestrator threads them into the next phase's prompt.
